steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: Use the form $h(x)=ab^x$. The row $x=0$ gives $h(0)=a=1.23$. Use the row $x=2$: $$1.54=1.23b^2$$ $$b^2=\frac{1.54}{1.23}\approx1.252$$ $$b\approx1.12.$$ So the model is $h(x)=1.23(1.12)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the model against the table
steps.content: The first value confirms the coefficient: when $x=0$, $h(0)=1.23(1.12)^0=1.23$. The second value checks the base: $1.23(1.12)^2 \approx 1.54$. The third value also matches: $1.23(1.12)^4 \approx 1.94$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $1.12(0.23)^x$: $h(0)=1.12$, not $1.23$; and $0.23$ would shrink the values. Eliminated.
  • B) $1.12(1.23)^x$: $h(0)=1.12$, not $1.23$. Eliminated.
  • C) $1.23(0.12)^x$: correct starting value, but $h(2)=1.23(0.12)^2\approx 0.018$, nowhere near $1.54$. Eliminated.
  • D) $1.23(1.12)^x$: $h(0)=1.23$, $h(2)\approx 1.54$, $h(4)\approx 1.94$. Matches all rows.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
